title: Introduction to Attrib
text: attrib provides way estimating mortality given exposures set reference value. using simulations posterior distribution coefficients can easily aggregate time locations still estimating valid credible intervals. vignette go : use fit_attrib fit model data use est_attrib estimate mortality different scenarios (.e. exposures reference values observed values) examples usages resulting dataset

title: Introduction to Attrib
text: use datasets data_fake_attrib_county data_fake_attrib_nation. data_fake_attrib_county consists fake mortality data counties Norway weekly basis 2010 2020. dataset consists following features: location_code: Location code different counties isoyear: Isoyear isoweek: Week number isoyearweek: Isoyear isoweek season: Years season seasonweek: Number weeks start season pop_jan1_n: Population size ili_isoweekmean0_6_pr100: Percentage doctors consultations diagnosed influenza like illnesses ili_isoweekmean7_13_pr100: ili_isoweekmean0_6_pr100 lagged one week heatwavedays_n: number heatwaves deaths_n: number deaths data_fake_attrib_nation similar dataset national level. example look exposures ili_isoweekmean7_13_pr100 heatwavedays_n calculate attributable mortality due exposures.
code: data_fake_county <- attrib::data_fake_attrib_county data_fake_nation <- attrib::data_fake_attrib_nation head(data_fake_county, 5) #>    location_code isoyear isoweek isoyearweek    season seasonweek pop_jan1_n #> 1:  county_nor03    2010       1     2010-01 2009/2010         24     693494 #> 2:  county_nor03    2011       1     2011-01 2010/2011         24     693494 #> 3:  county_nor03    2012       1     2012-01 2011/2012         24     693494 #> 4:  county_nor03    2013       1     2013-01 2012/2013         24     693494 #> 5:  county_nor03    2014       1     2014-01 2013/2014         24     693494 #>    ili_isoweekmean0_6_pr100 ili_isoweekmean7_13_pr100 heatwavedays_n deaths_n #> 1:                0.9231202                 0.8146507              0      112 #> 2:                1.8997241                 1.7890927              0      113 #> 3:                1.3924947                 1.6017501              0      128 #> 4:                0.9296033                 0.7167721              0      126 #> 5:                1.3933639                 1.1638354              0      115

title: Introduction to Attrib
text: want estimate attributable mortality due ILI heatwaves. attrib lets us fit models fixed random effect offsets using linear mixed models (LMM). use glmer function lme4 package. practice, means must specify response, offsets, fixed effects, random effects. case model response deaths function : heatwavedays_n ili_isoweekmean7_13_pr100 sin(2 * pi * (isoweek - 1) / 52) cos(2 * pi * (isoweek - 1) / 52) (1|location_code) (ili_isoweekmean7_13_pr100|season) log(pop_jan1_n) results following fit: Note fit added attributes offset (saving offset name) fit_fix (coefficients linear model fitted fixed effects). needed est_attrib create dataset containing fixed effects.

title: Introduction to Attrib
text: sim function can used generate simulations rows data. first generates n_sim simulations posterior distribution coefficients fit applying coefficients dataset generating n_sim simulations expected mortality line. quite generic. Hence goal compute attributable mortality incident risk ratios use est_attrib shown later part vignette. can see now multiple expected mortalities dataline. due coefficient simulations.

title: Introduction to Attrib
text: estimate attributable mortality simulate: estimated mortality observed exposures estimated mortality exposures set reference values easily done using est_attrib. need give fit, dataset, exposures reference values, number simulations. est_attrib using arm::sim function generate simulations underlying posterior distribution. attrib::sim combine simulated coefficients estimate modeled outcome (.e. number deaths) simulation. can see dataset columns id, sim_id, sim_value_exposures=observed, sim_value_heatwavedays_n=0, sim_value_ili_isoweekmean7_13_pr100=0 added previous set columns. row original dataset now 20  rows, one simulations done est_attrib. row see estimate number deaths given reference value sim_value_heatwavedays_n sim_value_ili_isoweekmean7_13_pr100. make data processing easier later convert dataset wide long form collapse estimated mortality can see columns sim_value_heatwavedays_n=0, sim_value_ili_isoweekmean7_13_pr100=0 now collapsed new column attr value attr describing exposure value giving corresponding reference value.

title: Introduction to Attrib
text: proceed aggregating county dataset national/seasonal level. Afterwards calculate expected attributable mortality, exp_attr, subtracting value (simulated expected number deaths given reference value exposure) sim_value_exposures=observed. able separate dataset add tag.
code: aggregated_county_to_nation <-  est_attrib_county_long[,.(   "sim_value_exposures=observed" = sum(`sim_value_exposures=observed`),   value = sum(value),    deaths_n = sum(deaths_n) ), keyby = .(season, attr, sim_id)]  # Add exp_attr, exp_irr and a tag. aggregated_county_to_nation[, exp_attr:= (`sim_value_exposures=observed` - value)] aggregated_county_to_nation[, tag := "aggregated_from_county"]  head(aggregated_county_to_nation, 5) #>       season                       attr sim_id sim_value_exposures=observed #> 1: 2009/2010 sim_value_heatwavedays_n=0      1                     44286.59 #> 2: 2009/2010 sim_value_heatwavedays_n=0      2                     44344.69 #> 3: 2009/2010 sim_value_heatwavedays_n=0      3                     43702.62 #> 4: 2009/2010 sim_value_heatwavedays_n=0      4                     44031.30 #> 5: 2009/2010 sim_value_heatwavedays_n=0      5                     44570.27 #>       value deaths_n exp_attr                    tag #> 1: 43887.26    44006 399.3274 aggregated_from_county #> 2: 43941.64    44006 403.0525 aggregated_from_county #> 3: 43283.10    44006 419.5210 aggregated_from_county #> 4: 43604.45    44006 426.8508 aggregated_from_county #> 5: 44177.14    44006 393.1318 aggregated_from_county

title: Introduction to Attrib
text: next thing aggregate away simulations. benefits simulations possibility gives efficiently compute desired quantiles. example use .05, .5 .95 quantiles. compute quantiles exp_attr following way. can now see credible intervals estimates attributable deaths exposures.
code: # Quantile functins q025 <- function(x){   return(quantile(x, 0.025)) } q975 <- function(x){   return(quantile(x, 0.975)) } col_names <- colnames(data_national) data.table::setkeyv(   data_national,    col_names[!col_names %in% c(     "exp_attr",      "sim_id",      "sim_value_exposures=observed",      "value",      "deaths_n"   )] )  aggregated_sim_seasonal_data_national<- data_national[   ,   unlist(     recursive = FALSE,      lapply(.(median = median, q025 = q025, q975 = q975), function(f) lapply(.SD, f))   ),    by = eval(data.table::key(data_national)),   .SDcols = c("exp_attr") ]  head(aggregated_sim_seasonal_data_national,5) #>       season                                  attr                    tag #> 1: 2009/2010            sim_value_heatwavedays_n=0 aggregated_from_county #> 2: 2009/2010            sim_value_heatwavedays_n=0                 nation #> 3: 2009/2010 sim_value_ili_isoweekmean7_13_pr100=0 aggregated_from_county #> 4: 2009/2010 sim_value_ili_isoweekmean7_13_pr100=0                 nation #> 5: 2010/2011            sim_value_heatwavedays_n=0 aggregated_from_county #>    median.exp_attr q025.exp_attr q975.exp_attr #> 1:        414.0397      385.3566      433.4521 #> 2:        432.6212      339.0910      504.1268 #> 3:        717.3795      551.7688      913.0322 #> 4:        720.3073      514.1911      885.5093 #> 5:        461.6406      431.7522      485.3322

what: Plot to compare the national with the aggregated county to national model
title: Introduction to Attrib
text: able compare two models make point range plot using ggplot2.
code: q <- ggplot(   aggregated_sim_seasonal_data_national[attr == "sim_value_ili_isoweekmean7_13_pr100=0"],    aes(x = season, y = median.exp_attr, group = tag, color = tag) ) q <- q + geom_pointrange(   aes(x = season, y = median.exp_attr, ymin = q025.exp_attr, ymax = q975.exp_attr),    position = position_dodge(width = 0.3)   ) q <- q + ggtitle("Attributable mortality due to ILI in Norway according to 2 models")  q <- q +  scale_y_continuous("Estimated attributable mortality")  q <- q +  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90),axis.title.x=element_blank())  q <- q +  labs(caption = glue::glue("Aggregated county model: Attributable mortality modeled on a county level before beeing aggregated up to a national level.\n National model: Attributable mortality modeled on a national level.")) q

title: Introduction to Attrib

title: Nowcasting with Attrib
text: data frame either generated nowcast_aggregate, form shown , can use nowcast correct delay registration. set many week want adjust. “n_week_adjust” = 5 current week prior 4 weeks corrected. Meaning 5 weeks total. also set many weeks want train model . Note assume weeks weeks state adjusted true data. words assume 100 % deaths present. doubt set “n_week_adjust” bit higher. case seen plots 5 weeks almost 100 % data registered therefor chose correct 5 weeks. use default functions “nowcast_correction_fn” nowcast_correction_fn_negbin_mm, however can manually set long full fill certain conditions. function must following arguments: data, dataset generated nowcast_aggregate form n_week_adjusting, number weeks adjustd offset date_0, last date dataset. function must return vector containing following: data, corrected data set containing columns ncor, ncor0_1 ncor0_n_week_adjusting n_week_adjusting number weeks adjusted fit <- vector containgin fit correction, (one per week n_week_adjusting). also use default simulation function nowcast_correction_sim_neg_bin. can also set manually must contain following arguments: nowcast_correction_object made “nowcast_correction_fn” offset, n_sim, number simulations date_0, last date data set. must return following dataset containing following rows: yrwk, yearweek n_death, number observed deaths sim_value, predicted number deaths one simulation cut_doe, first date every week week year location_code sim_id, simmulation id also important make sure simulation function works together chosen correction function. nowcast returns two datasets. One original dataset median corrected data added containing simulations predicted value ncor one can make credible intervals.

title: Nowcasting with Attrib
text: evaluate estimates made nowcast evaluation function made. function takes “nowcast_object” number weeks adjust “n_week_adjusting” arguments. can evaluate residual plots make sure bias. see residualplot n corrected week 2.  resuduals show bias look accuratcy predictions using absolute error (“abs_error”), \(R^2\) (“R_squared”), mean square error (“MSE”), root mean square error (“RMSE”).

title: Statistical properties used in Attrib
text: package builds Baysian framework general knowledge assumed. goal model attributable mortality definition given . model number deaths (\(n_{death}\)) function given exposures represented matrix \(X\). default version sim function used est_attrib calculates attributable mortality following way. use fit made fit_attrib using lme4s glmer function produce fit data assuming data poison distributed mean \(\lambda\) link function \(\eta = X \beta = log(\lambda)\). use compute posterior distribution \(\beta\) using sim function arm package. assumes posterior distribution \(\beta\) normal distribution using coefficients correlation fit compute mean variance posterior distribution. leads us \(n\) simulations posterior distribution \(\beta\) corresponding \(n\) simulations expected value \(n_{death_i}\), \(E(n_{death_i}) = e^{X \beta_i}\) now define attributable mortality : Attributable mortality () := expected number deaths (n_death) given exposures - expected number deaths exposure set reference value rest unchanged. mathematical terms \[ AM_{exposure_i} := E(n_{death_i}| X) - E(n_{death_i}| x_i = ref, x_{j \neq } \text{ unchanged}) \] values aggregated end credible interval attributable mortality.

title: Statistical properties used in Attrib
text: case baseline estimation mortality model fitted data assuming quasipoisson distributed mean \(\lambda\) link function . sim function arm package used simulate values \(\beta\) posterior distribution. use sample quasipoison distribution get samples \(n_{death_i}\). obtain \(n_{sim}\) values \(n_{death_i}\) time point \(n_{death_i} \sim quasipoison(X \beta_i)\). aggregating simulations leads credible interval \(n_{death}\) every week every year.
